guidance.text:
Knowledge Summary: Repeating LFTs
The purpose of repeating LFT is to assess trends and have confirmation for a diagnosis
• To make a diagnosis trends need to be present for at least 3 months
• The ALT should not exceed 3 x upper limit of normal (suggests alternative pathology)
• ALT would be comparatively higher than AST
• If worry or concern for Abx induced injury etc may want to repeat in 2-4 weeks, if picked up routinely can simply repeat in 3 months

Knowledge Summary: Initial consultation for liver pathology
During this consultation we inform the patient:
• We have found sustained abnormalities in the liver tests
• We have not arrived at a definitive answer or diagnosis nor are we currently able to give a diagnosis currently
• We have a need to explore further to arrive at a diagnosis

We can tell them that this is commonly due to lifestyle and metabolic causes but we need to exclude more concerning conditions such as hepatitis. We should inform them these are very rare and treated by specialists if diagnosed.

Most patients can make significant improvements, and some manage to entirely reverse the changes, by:
• Losing weight
• Lowering cholesterol
• Lowering A1C
• Lowering blood pressure
• Lowering alcohol intake

At this point ask the patient about things which would be relevant to significant liver disease:
• Confusion
• Ascites and peripheral oedema in general
• If male, testicular atrophy or gynaecomastia
guidance.location: Green box, left margin, middle section
guidance.linked_to: Patient needs consultation for liver pathology and consent for further testing

guidance.text:
Knowledge Summary: Additional testing
During this consultation we inform the patient:
• We have found sustained abnormalities in the liver tests
• We have not arrived at a definitive answer or diagnosis
• The additional testing is often negative but allows us to rule out rarer causes which might require specialist input
• It is reasonable to say that 'if the USS shows nothing sinister and the additional tests are normal we are likely looking at a metabolic cause of abnormal LFTs', which is something we would discuss with them later
guidance.location: Green box, left margin, lower section

guidance.text:
Baseline Liver Profile: LFT, FBC, AST, GGT (To allow FIB4 Calculation)

Phase 2 Bloods
This Phase needs to set the groundwork for further testing/work up in the future.

Here we need to ensure there has been the following tests within 12 months:
• Coeliac Screen
• Iron Profile
• HBA1C
• Ferritin
• Lipids
• TSH
• Hepatitis Screen A, B, C and E
• Immunoglobulins
• HIV
• ANCA
guidance.subworkflow_brief: Subworkflow: Phase 2 Bloods Checklist. Input: Patient with suspected liver pathology entering Phase 2. Step-by-step logic: (1) Order Baseline Liver Profile: LFT, FBC, AST, GGT to enable FIB4 calculation. (2) Check whether each of the following tests has been performed within the last 12 months: Coeliac Screen, Iron Profile, HBA1C, Ferritin, Lipids, TSH, Hepatitis Screen A/B/C/E, Immunoglobulins, HIV, ANCA. (3) If any test is missing or older than 12 months, order it. Output: Complete baseline panel confirming all required tests are current, enabling progression to Phase 3/4 assessment.

guidance.text:
NAFLD Fibrosis Score Categories
< 1.455: F0-F2 (no to moderate fibrosis)
-1.455 to 0.675: Indeterminate Score
≥ 0.675: F3-F4 (Severe fibrosis or Cirrhosis)
guidance.location: Blue box, right-centre area, below Phase 4 Bloods node
guidance.subworkflow_brief: Subworkflow: NAFLD Fibrosis Score Classification. Input: Calculated NAFLD Fibrosis Score numeric value. Step-by-step logic: (1) If score < -1.455 → classify as F0-F2 (no to moderate fibrosis) → manage risk factors and co-morbidities with Annual Screening. (2) If score between -1.455 and 0.675 → classify as Indeterminate Score → proceed to Fibrous Scan if available. (3) If score ≥ 0.675 → classify as F3-F4 (Severe fibrosis or Cirrhosis) → refer to Phase 4 Bloods then Secondary Care. Output: Fibrosis category determining next management pathway.

guidance.text:
FIB 4 Categories
< 1.3 (or <2.0 if age ≥65): Low Risk → manage risk factors and co-morbidities - Annual Screening
1.3–2.67 (or 2.0–2.67 if age ≥65): Intermediate or High Risk → Fibrous Scan if available
>2.67 (at any age): High Risk → Phase 4 Bloods
guidance.subworkflow_brief: Subworkflow: FIB-4 Score Classification and Routing. Input: Calculated FIB-4 numeric value and patient age. Step-by-step logic: (1) If FIB-4 < 1.3 (or < 2.0 if age ≥ 65) → Low Risk → manage risk factors and co-morbidities with Annual Screening. (2) If FIB-4 between 1.3 and 2.67 (or 2.0 and 2.67 if age ≥ 65) → Intermediate or High Risk → proceed to Fibrous Scan if available. (3) If FIB-4 > 2.67 (at any age) → High Risk → proceed to Phase 4 Bloods. Output: Risk category and next management step.

guidance.text:
Mandatory Testing:
• Wilsons Disease Screen: Ceruloplasmin, Alpha 1 Antitrypsin (A1AT) Screen, A1AT
• Transferrin Sat ≥45% Or Raised Ferritin → Haemochromatosis Gene Test
• Raised IgM → Primary Biliary Cirrhosis Screen: AMA
guidance.location: Centre of flowchart, below Phase 4 Bloods, left branch
guidance.subworkflow_brief: Subworkflow: Mandatory Phase 4 Testing Protocol. Input: Patient entering Phase 4 mandatory testing. Step-by-step logic: (1) Always order Wilsons Disease Screen including Ceruloplasmin and Alpha 1 Antitrypsin (A1AT) Screen with A1AT level. (2) Check Transferrin Saturation: if ≥45% or Ferritin is raised → order Haemochromatosis Gene Test. (3) Check IgM level: if raised → order Primary Biliary Cirrhosis Screen including AMA. Output: Completed mandatory rare disease screening panel with triggered conditional tests based on results.

guidance.text:
Conditional Testing:
• Ulcerative Colitis or P-ANCA positive → Requires MRCP via Gastroenterology
• Raised IgG → Autoimmune Hepatitis Type 1 Screen: ANA, SMA; Autoimmune Hepatitis Type 2 Screen: LKM-1, SLA (not on EMIS)
guidance.location: Centre of flowchart, below Phase 4 Bloods, right branch under Conditional Testing node
guidance.subworkflow_brief: Subworkflow: Conditional Phase 4 Testing Protocol. Input: Patient Phase 4 conditional test triggers (history of Ulcerative Colitis, P-ANCA result, IgG level). Step-by-step logic: (1) Check for Ulcerative Colitis history or P-ANCA positive result: if either present → refer for MRCP via Gastroenterology. (2) Check IgG level: if raised → order Autoimmune Hepatitis Type 1 Screen (ANA, SMA) AND Autoimmune Hepatitis Type 2 Screen (LKM-1, SLA — note SLA not on EMIS). Output: Conditional specialist referrals and autoimmune hepatitis screening results.

guidance.text:
FASL–EASD–EASO Clinical Practice Guidelines on the management of metabolic dysfunction-associated steatotic liver disease (MASLD) - Journal of Hepatology
Graphs and Images.

llm_response:
The **Liver Pathology Phased Work Up** workflow is fully built and validated ✅ — **42 nodes, 49 connections**, covering the complete clinical pathway:

---

### What was built:

**Phase 1 & 2 → Tests Normalise?**
- ✅ **Yes** → Mark as Normal/Satisfactory

**Not Normalised → Findings ≥3 months?**
- ✅ **Yes** → Phase 3 Recheck → normalise? → Normal end *or* Consultation
- ✅ **No (<3 months)** → Calculate FIB4 & NAFLD Score → normal? → "Code as normal USS" *or* Request Liver USS → Consultation

**Consultation → Phase 4 Bloods → 4 parallel branches:**
1. **Mandatory Testing** — Wilson's Disease Screen, conditional Haemochromatosis Gene Test, conditional PBC Screen (IgM)
2. **Conditional Testing** — MRCP (UC/P-ANCA), Autoimmune Hepatitis Screens (IgG)
3. **NAFLD Fibrosis Score Categories** — F0-F2 (Annual Screening) / Indeterminate → Fibrous Scan / F3-F4 (Secondary Care)
4. **FIB4 Categories** — Low Risk (Annual Screening) / Intermediate → Fibrous Scan / High Risk (Secondary Care)

**All paths converge on:** Review Result → Telephone Consultation → Referral decision → **Secondary Care** or **No Concern/Escalation Required**

---

Would you like me to convert any sections into subworkflows (e.g. Phase 2 Bloods checklist, NAFLD/FIB4 scoring, Mandatory Testing protocol)?
